edibility: Premier edible — must be cooked thoroughly (raw morels contain hydrazine toxins); often combined with cream sauces; never eaten raw
taste: Earthy, nutty, smoky umami; honeycomb cap holds sauce; firm bite
search: Where/when to find — early/mid-spring, floodplains, orchards, burn scars, old elm trees; pitted (true) cap fully attached to stem; DEADLY look-alikes (Gyromitra false morels) — false morels are lobed/wavy not pitted, and stem is cottony-filled rather than hollow

toxicity.symptoms: Raw morels: GI symptoms (hydrazine); also alcohol intolerance reported
toxicity.treatmentWindow: Seek medical attention if symptoms persist beyond 24 hours
toxicity.antidote: Supportive care; avoid alcohol when eating morels
